questions: Should the bounding box visually span the full track height or just the clip height (track height minus the 4px vertical margins)?
What should happen when clips are being actively dragged or resized — should the bounding box update in real-time, disappear temporarily, or stay at its original position?
Should there be any label or indicator showing the shot name on the bounding box, or is it purely a colored border/background?
What is the adjacency tolerance — should clips with a tiny gap (e.g., 1 frame at 30fps = 0.033s) still be considered adjacent, or must they be exactly touching?

success_criteria.criterion: Colored bounding boxes render around groups of 2+ adjacent same-track clips that belong to the same shot in image-array order
Bounding boxes use pointer-events-none and do not interfere with clip selection, dragging, resizing, or context menus
Each shot gets a deterministic color derived from its ID that is stable across re-renders
Multiple shots can have visible bounding boxes simultaneously with different colors
Bounding boxes only appear for clips on the same track (not cross-track)
Bounding boxes only appear for strictly adjacent clips (no gaps)
Bounding boxes only appear when clips are in the same order as the shot's images array
Bounding box positions update correctly when clips are resized (via resizeOverrides)
All existing timeline tests continue to pass
New unit tests cover core algorithm: adjacency, ordering, cross-track, single-clip, mixed shots, deterministic color
Hook computation is memoized and only recomputes when timeline data or shots change
Bounding box visual is subtle (low-opacity background, semi-transparent border) and does not obscure clip content
Color palette has at least 6 visually distinct colors that work on the dark timeline background

assumptions: Clips are considered 'adjacent' if the gap between them is less than ~1ms (0.001s) — purely a floating-point tolerance, not a musical/video frame concept
A qualifying group requires a minimum of 2 clips — a single clip from a shot does not get a bounding box
The bounding box is rendered BEHIND clips (z-index below clip layer) as a background highlight, not on top
Shot images order is determined by the array index in shot.images[], not by any 'position' or 'timeline_frame' field
Clips that belong to a shot but have no generationId in their asset registry entry are ignored (treated as not belonging to any shot)
The bounding box spans the full row height (including vertical margins) to create a clear visual grouping, but this is a question above
